Question Text: The Saturn V rockets that carried astronauts to the Moon were designed in the city of Huntsville, which lies in which state, also remembered as the place where Rosa Parks sparked the Montgomery bus boycott?
Sled-dog teams race the long Iditarod trail beneath Denali, the tallest peak in North America, in which giant state, also known as the 'Last Frontier' whose capital Juneau has no roads in or out?
The giant saguaro cacti of the Sonoran Desert, where the Navajo and Hopi have long lived, grow in which state, also famous for the mile-deep Grand Canyon carved by the Colorado River?

Visitors dig for and keep real gems at the Crater of Diamonds, the only public diamond mine in the country, in which 'Natural State', also known as the place where Sam Walton opened the first Walmart?
The scorching, below-sea-level depths of Death Valley sit in which state, also known as the 'Golden State' of the 1849 Gold Rush, the Golden Gate Bridge, and Hollywood?
The ancient stone cliff dwellings of Mesa Verde cling to the Rocky Mountains in which state, also known for its 'Mile High City' capital of Denver, exactly one mile above sea level?
The world's first nuclear-powered submarine was launched in which small New England state, also known as the insurance capital, home to the centuries-old Yale University?
More than a million businesses choose to legally register in which tiny state, also proudly known as the 'First State' for being first to ratify the U.S. Constitution?
Rockets blast off from the Kennedy Space Center in which state, also known as the 'Sunshine State' of Walt Disney World and the alligator-filled Everglades?
The soft drink Coca-Cola was invented in the city of Atlanta in which state, also known as the 'Peach State' and the birthplace of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.?
The active Kilauea volcano keeps erupting in which island state, also famous as the 'Aloha State' of Waikiki surf beaches and the Pearl Harbor naval base?
Hells Canyon, a gorge even deeper than the Grand Canyon, slices along the edge of which state, also known as the 'Gem State' famous for growing potatoes?
The world's very first skyscraper rose in Chicago in which state, also known as the 'Land of Lincoln' and home to the towering Willis Tower?
Hundreds of thousands of fans pack a legendary oval track for the Indianapolis 500 in which state, also known for its basketball-loving people called Hoosiers?
A baseball diamond carved into a cornfield inspired the movie 'Field of Dreams' in which state, also known as the corn-and-pork 'Hawkeye State' that holds the first presidential caucus?
Dorothy's farmhouse was swept away by a twister in 'The Wizard of Oz' in which Tornado Alley state, also known as the flat, wheat-growing 'Sunflower State'?
Mammoth Cave, the longest cave system in the world, winds beneath which state, also famous for the Kentucky Derby horse race and the gold vault at Fort Knox?
Jazz music was born in the city of New Orleans in which swampy state, also known as the 'Pelican State' that throws the giant Mardi Gras festival each year?
Acadia National Park is among the first places in the country to see the morning sunrise in which northeastern state, also famous for its lobster and rocky lighthouses?
The flag flying over Fort McHenry inspired the words of the national anthem in which state, also known for the U.S. Naval Academy and its steamed blue crabs?
The Pilgrims first stepped ashore at Plymouth Rock in which state, also famous for the Boston Tea Party and Harvard, the oldest college in the country?
Four of the five Great Lakes wrap around which mitten-shaped state, also known as the 'Motor City' home of Detroit's car factories and Motown music?
The mighty Mississippi River trickles to life from a small lake in which state, also known as the 'Land of 10,000 Lakes' with its enormous Mall of America?
The soulful Delta blues was born along the river that shares its name in which Deep South state, also known as the 'Magnolia State' where Elvis Presley was born in Tupelo?
Pioneer wagon trails such as the Oregon Trail set out westward from which state, also famous for the 630-foot Gateway Arch towering over St. Louis?
The Battle of Little Bighorn, where Custer made his last stand, was fought in which state, also known as 'Big Sky Country' and home to Glacier National Park?
Oregon Trail pioneers once steered by the tall stone spire of Chimney Rock across which flat state, also known as the corn-growing 'Cornhusker State'?
The secretive military base known as Area 51 hides in the desert of which state, also known as the 'Silver State' that glitters with the neon Las Vegas Strip?
The nation's very first presidential primary is held every four years in which small state, also known as the 'Granite State' whose bold motto is 'Live Free or Die'?
The streets on the classic Monopoly board were named after the seaside resort of Atlantic City in which state, also known as the crowded yet green 'Garden State'?
The world's first atomic bomb was secretly tested in the desert of which state, also known as the 'Land of Enchantment' famous for the Roswell UFO legend?
Wall Street's stock exchange and the bright lights of Times Square fill the giant city of which state, also famous for the Statue of Liberty and Niagara Falls?
The Wright Brothers made the world's first powered airplane flight on the dunes of Kitty Hawk in which 'Tar Heel State', also proud to call itself 'First in Flight'?
The Badlands once roamed by a young Theodore Roosevelt stretch across which state, also known as the wheat-and-oil 'Peace Garden State' sitting on the Canadian border?
Astronaut Neil Armstrong and the airplane-inventing Wright Brothers were all born in which state, also known as the 'Buckeye State' and home of the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame?
Settlers who sneaked in early to grab land became known as 'Sooners' in which state, also known for its many Native American nations and historic Route 66?
Crater Lake, the deepest lake in the country, fills a collapsed volcano in which state, also known as the 'Beaver State' where the Oregon Trail finally ended?
The Declaration of Independence was signed in the city of Philadelphia in which state, also famous for the cracked Liberty Bell and Hershey's chocolate?
The grand Gilded Age mansions of Newport line a sparkling bay in which state, also known as the smallest state of all, the 'Ocean State'?
The very first shots of the Civil War were fired at Fort Sumter in which state, also known as the 'Palmetto State' of historic, cobblestoned Charleston?
The Crazy Horse Memorial and the eerie Badlands lie in which state, also world-famous for the four presidents' faces carved into Mount Rushmore?
Elvis Presley's Graceland mansion draws fans to Memphis in which state, also known as the 'Volunteer State' and the home of country music in Nashville?
NASA's astronaut 'Mission Control' is based in Houston in which state, also known as the enormous 'Lone Star State' whose defenders made a last stand at the Alamo?
The racing-fast Bonneville Salt Flats and the super-salty Great Salt Lake lie in which state, also known as the 'Beehive State' settled by Mormon pioneers?
The Ben & Jerry's ice-cream factory is a favorite stop in which small New England state, also known as the 'Green Mountain State' that makes the most maple syrup?
Jamestown, the first lasting English colony in America, was founded in which state, also known as the 'Mother of Presidents' and home of the Pentagon?
Mount St. Helens erupted in a huge blast in 1980 in which state, also known as the 'Evergreen State' home of the Space Needle, Boeing jets, and Starbucks coffee?
The soaring New River Gorge Bridge arches across which coal-mining state, also known as the 'Mountain State' that split away from Virginia during the Civil War?
Football fans wear foam 'cheesehead' hats to cheer the Green Bay Packers in which state, also proudly known as 'America's Dairyland'?
Old Faithful erupts on schedule inside Yellowstone, the world's first national park, in which least-populated state, also famous as the first to let women vote?
